data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning ConocoPhillips Stock In The Last 5 Years
data.description:

data.title: He Was Skeptical Towards 'Passive Income,' But This Full-Time Engineer Built A $1,500 A Month Side Hustle With 5 ATMs And 3 Vending Machines
data.description:
That search eventually led him to something surprisingly simple: placing ATMs in local businesses. Today, Butterton and a business partner run a small operation with five ATMs and three vending machines, generating about $1,500 a month in profit.

Don't Miss:

Building A Low-Maintenance Side Hustle

When Butterton first came across ATM businesses on social media, it seemed almost too straightforward.

“The more I looked into it, the more I was like, ‘OK, there’s really no risk in trying this, outside that original financial obligation of $2,500 to buy the ATM,'” he told BI.

While he doesn't fully buy into the idea of passive income, he admits that this comes close.

“You’re only there for 10 minutes to load it up, and then you’re on your way,” Butterton said. “It’s essentially your employer that you don’t have to pay.”

He and his partner launched their business in Silverdale, Washington, in 2022. Over time, they scaled up by adding more machines, including some secondhand ATMs they picked up for as little as $1,000 each.

Getting started isn't free, though. Butterton estimates about $5,000 per ATM when factoring in the machine, the cash needed to stock it, and a reserve buffer.

Trending: Avoid the #1 Investing Mistake: How Your ‘Safe' Holdings Could Be Costing You Big Time 

How The ATM Business Actually Makes Money

The business model is straightforward: every time someone withdraws cash, they pay a fee.

Whenever someone pays the out-of-network fee, all of that money goes straight into the business account, Butterton told BI. “That’s your profit, and it’s purely location-based.”

That last part is key. Location can make or break the business. Butterton targets places where people are more likely to need cash quickly, such as barbershops, convenience stores, and nail salons. Cash-only businesses are especially valuable.

He found that setting the right fee also matters. Starting at $2.50, he eventually realized that about $3 per transaction worked best in his market.

Butterton’s rule of thumb is simple: around 100 transactions a month at a $3 fee can bring in roughly $300 per machine.

“If you have good locations, that’s a very achievable number,” he told BI.

Not every location is easy to land. Cannabis dispensaries, for example, can be highly profitable due to heavy cash usage, Butterton said, but competition is intense and usually requires sharing revenue with the business.

The Challenges Most People Overlook

Despite the simplicity, there are real hurdles.

One of the biggest is banking. Many large banks avoid working with ATM operators due to concerns about fraud and money laundering. Butterton had to find a smaller local bank willing to support his business.

There's also the risk of theft and damage. In four years, one of his machines was stolen and later found empty on the side of a highway.

“When that happens, it’s a setback, and it might knock you off the track that you were on for a little bit, but if everything is set up correctly, you recover,” Butterton told BI. “That’s part of the business. You have to assume that that’s going to happen at some point.”

In the end, Butterton's side hustle is enough for him. The extra cash helps cover everyday expenses like a car payment and child care, without taking away from time at home.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Constellation Brands Stock Fizzles After Q4 Earnings: Here's Why
data.description:
• STZ stock is moving. Watch the price action here.

The Details: Constellation Brands, home of the Modelo and Corona beer brands, reported quarterly earnings of $1.90 per share, which beat the consensus estimate of $1.71.  

Quarterly revenue came in at $1.92 billion, which beat the Street estimate of $1.86 billion and was down from $2.16 billion in the same period last year, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Outlook: Constellation Brands expects fiscal 2027 adjusted EPS of $11.20 to $11.90, versus the $12.37 analyst estimate.

STZ Stock Price: According to data from Benzinga Pro, Constellation Brands stock fell 2% to $147.26 in Wednesday's extended trading.  

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Applied Digital Delivers Strong Q3 Results, Sees 'Clear Acceleration' In AI Data Center Demand
data.description:
• Applied Digital shares are trending after earnings. What’s driving APLD stock?

Applied Digital Q3 Highlights

Total revenue was up 139% on a year-over-year basis, primarily driven by growth in the company’s HPC hosting business combined with performance improvements in other segments.

“We now operate one of the only 100 MW direct-to-chip liquid-cooled data centers online today, and more importantly, it is fully operational,” said Wes Cummins, chairman and CEO of Applied Digital.

“We believe that’s what matters to our customers — turning power into live AI capacity, delivered on time and performing as expected. We are also starting to see the earnings power of our platform come through, with a full quarter of revenue from our first building now recognized.”

Applied Digital ended the quarter with approximately $2.1 billion in cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash, and $2.7 billion of total debt.

What’s Next For Applied Digital?

Construction remains on schedule across Applied Digital’s North Dakota campuses. ELN-2 at Polaris Forge 1 is now fully operational and the next two 150 megawatt facilities are continuing to advance. At Polaris Forge 2, the company said both buildings are progressing well with foundations largely complete.

Applied Digital also broke ground on Delta Forge 1 during the quarter. The company expects initial operations to begin at that location in mid-2027.

“We are seeing a clear acceleration in demand for high-performance AI data center capacity, with hyperscalers as aggressive as we have ever seen them,” Cummins said.

Applied Digital executives will further discuss the quarter on an earnings call scheduled for 5 p.m. ET.

APLD Shares Rise After The Bell

APLD Price Action: Applied Digital shares were up 2.37% in after-hours, trading at $28.45 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock.com
